title: 8 Herbal Creams For Neuritis
intro_desc: Herbal creams for neuritis are becoming increasingly popular as an alternative to traditional treatments. Neuritis is a condition where the nerves become inflamed, leading to pain, numbness, and tingling sensations in the body. Herbal teas, such as those made from Zingiber officinale (ginger), Ginkgo biloba, and Echinacea purpurea, can help relieve neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Ginger, for example, contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help ease nerve pain and discomfort. Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve blood flow to the nerves, which can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations. Echinacea purpurea, on the other hand, is believed to have antioxidant properties that can help protect the nerves from damage. When applied topically as a cream, these herbs can be absorbed into the skin, where they can provide localized relief from neuritis symptoms. This can be especially beneficial for people who experience neuritis in specific areas of the body, such as the hands or feet. By using herbal creams for neuritis, people can find relief from the pain and discomfort associated with this condition, allowing them to resume their daily activities and enjoy a better quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains bioactive constituents like gingerols and shogaols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can damage nerve cells. Additionally, the warming effect of Zingiber officinale creams helps to increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing numbness and tingling sensations. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Zingiber officinale creams can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 1 cup of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of ginger root powder to the double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Stir in 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a bowl and let it cool. Once cooled, transfer it to a container and store in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The flavonoids, in particular, have antioxidant properties that protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals. Bilobalide and ginkgolides help improve blood flow to the nerves, which is essential for nerve function and regeneration. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba extract, shea butter, and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool until it solidifies.
Once the mixture is solid, stir it well and transfer it into a jar or container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, glycoproteins, and caffeic acid derivatives. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and damage to nerve tissue. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, helping to shield nerve cells from damage and promote their health. Glycoproteins, on the other hand, have been found to have a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing pain and discomfort associated with neuritis. By combining these properties, Echinacea purpurea creams may provide relief from the symptoms of neuritis, promoting healing and recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Infuse coconut oil with Echinacea purpurea flowers by mixing them in a clean glass jar and leaving it in a sunny place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the coconut oil from the Echinacea purpurea flowers through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler and mix it with the infused coconut oil, vitamin E oil, and lavender oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and harden. Store the cream in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and protect nerve cells from damage. The triterpenoid saponins, specifically, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of neuritis. The flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant effects that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, a common factor in neuritis. By reducing inflammation and protecting nerve cells, Calendula officinalis creams may provide relief from the symptoms of neuritis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the dried Calendula flowers in the coconut oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the Calendula flowers.

Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth. Add the infused oil and vitamin E oil, stirring well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Calendula cream for neuritis is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains active constituents like flavonoids, naphthodianthrones, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and damage in neuritis. The flavonoids, specifically hyperoside and quercetin, have been shown to have a protective effect on nerve cells. The naphthodianthrones, including hyperforin, have been found to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the spread of infection that may cause neuritis. The combination of these properties in Hypericum perforatum creams makes it a useful treatment for neuritis.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the melted oil and beeswax mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix in the shea butter and vitamin E oil until well combined. Pour the mixture into a jar and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the nerves, making them beneficial for treating neuritis. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to nerve damage. The flavonoids in Achillea millefolium creams also have antioxidant properties that help protect the nerves from oxidative stress and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Achillea millefolium creams can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Combine the dried herbs with 2 cups of carrier oil (like olive or coconut oil) in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains essential oils that have been traditionally used to help with neuritis. The main bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis are rosmarinic acid and luteolin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which can contribute to neuritis. Additionally, the essential oils in Melissa officinalis creams have a calming effect and can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis creams may help to alleviate some of the symptoms of neuritis.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, dried or fresh.
Combine 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers with 2 cups of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and then transfer it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata creams contains boswellic acids, specifically akkalic acid and 11-keto-beta-boswellic acid, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with neuritis. The boswellic acids in Boswellia serrata creams also exhibit antioxidant properties, which can protect nerve cells from damage and promote healing. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Boswellia serrata creams may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, leading to improved nerve function. The combination of these properties makes Boswellia serrata creams a potentially effective treatment for neuritis.
remedies.image_alt: Boswellia serrata herbal creams for neuritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Boswellia serrata powder, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add Boswellia serrata powder and stir well to combine. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the powder is fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and stir in shea butter and vitamin E oil until smooth. Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify completely. Once set, scoop out and use as a cream for neuritis relief. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Some teas, like ginger and turmeric, contain anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated nerves. Others, like chamomile, can calm the nervous system and promote overall well-being. Regular consumption may help alleviate symptoms and prevent neuritis from forming.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for neuritis can be a soothing option, but it's essential to be cautious with daily consumption. Some herbs, like ginseng and ashwagandha, can interact with medications or worsen symptoms if taken excessively. Moderation is key, so start with small amounts and observe how your body responds.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for neuritis can start working within a few days to a week. Some people notice improvements in their nerve pain and numbness after drinking these teas regularly for about 7-14 days. The exact time frame may vary depending on the individual and the specific herbal tea used.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for neuritis is best in the morning. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in the tea can help reduce nerve pain and inflammation throughout the day. Sipping it as soon as you wake up can give you a boost and prepare your body for the day ahead.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for neuritis. Some herbs, like feverfew and ginkgo biloba, may slow blood clotting and increase the risk of bleeding. Others, such as St. John's Wort, can affect the way the body processes certain medications. This may cause unwanted side effects or reduce the effectiveness of the prescribed treatment.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have neuritis, a herbal tea might sound like a soothing solution. However, some herbal teas can be too strong for your baby. For example, peppermint or ginger tea can cause stomach problems if consumed in excess. It's best to stick with mild, gentle options like chamomile tea, but check the ingredients first to be sure they're safe for you and your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: While breastfeeding, it's generally safe to consume herbal teas for neuritis as long as you choose ones that are considered safe during lactation. Peppermint, chamomile, and ginger teas are often used for neuritis and are also okay to drink while breastfeeding. However, some herbal teas like sage and pennyroyal should be avoided.
